query:primary_minus_line_at_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested signed difference as an integer
query:primary_minus_line_at_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "primary_mark" and "line_mark"; "primary_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the primary-series mark at the queried category, and "line_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the overlaid line mark at the same category

query:line_minus_primary_at_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested signed difference as an integer
query:line_minus_primary_at_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "primary_mark" and "line_mark"; "primary_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the primary-series mark at the queried category, and "line_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the overlaid line mark at the same category

query:max_line_where_primary_above_thres.answer_hint: set "answer" to the exact visible category label as a string
query:max_line_where_primary_above_thres.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "primary_mark" and "line_mark"; "primary_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the primary-series mark at the answer category, and "line_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the overlaid line mark at the same answer category

query:min_line_where_primary_above_thres.answer_hint: set "answer" to the exact visible category label as a string
query:min_line_where_primary_above_thres.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "primary_mark" and "line_mark"; "primary_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the primary-series mark at the answer category, and "line_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the overlaid line mark at the same answer category

query:max_primary_where_line_below_thres.answer_hint: set "answer" to the exact visible category label as a string
query:max_primary_where_line_below_thres.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "primary_mark" and "line_mark"; "primary_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the primary-series mark at the answer category, and "line_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the overlaid line mark at the same answer category

query:min_primary_where_line_below_thres.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "primary_mark" and "line_mark"; "primary_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the primary-series mark at the answer category, and "line_mark" is a [x,y] pixel point on the overlaid line mark at the same answer category

query:primary_above_and_line_above.answer_hint: set "answer" to the number of matching categories as an integer
query:primary_above_and_line_above.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a list of segments. Each segment is two [x, y] pixel points formatted [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]], using the primary-series mark point and the overlaid line mark point for one matching category; use [] if no categories match

query:primary_above_and_line_below.answer_hint: set "answer" to the number of matching categories as an integer
query:primary_above_and_line_below.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a list of segments. Each segment is two [x, y] pixel points formatted [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]], using the primary-series mark point and the overlaid line mark point for one matching category; use [] if no categories match

query:primary_below_and_line_above.answer_hint: set "answer" to the number of matching categories as an integer
query:primary_below_and_line_above.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a list of segments. Each segment is two [x, y] pixel points formatted [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]], using the primary-series mark point and the overlaid line mark point for one matching category; use [] if no categories match

query:primary_between_and_line_above.answer_hint: set "answer" to the number of matching categories as an integer

query:line_between_and_primary_above.answer_hint: set "answer" to the number of matching categories as an integer
